title: spergula australis
intro: Spergula australis, also known as Australian wood sorrel, is a perennial herb native to Australia and New Zealand, belonging to the family Amaranthaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Spergula australis include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation and pain, and support the immune system. Spergula australis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
